sections.paragraph:
- Original: στοιχέω

- Transliteration: Stoicheo

- Phonetic: stoy-kheh'-o

- Definition:

1. to proceed in a row as the march of a soldier, go in order

a. metaph. to go on prosperously, to turn out well

2. to walk

a. to direct one's life, to live

- Origin: from a derivative of steicho (to range in regular line)

- TDNT entry: 18:06,1

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: From a derivative of στείχω steichō̄ (to range in regular line); to march in (military) rank (keep step) that is (figuratively) to conform to virtue and piety: - walk (orderly).
